phases.title: Register decision + token foundation
phases.summary: Confirmed the iOS register pivot, established IOS_REGISTER tokens and text recipes, and rewrote the token foundation so later surface work could share a single visual base.

phases.title: Single-source-of-truth chrome flips
phases.summary: Flipped shared chrome primitives such as tab headers, segmented controls, pill tabs, and active-state accents to the iOS register so multiple tabs changed through central components.

phases.title: Build the new iOS-register components
phases.summary: Built the reusable iOS-register component kit and sandbox route, including BeatCard, QuoteCard, ForecastTileGroup, PermissionRuleCallout, CoralAIPromptCard, WorkingOnPill, ToolbarComposer, and CrewList.

phases.title: Wire Race Prep surface
phases.summary: Wired the first full iOS-register step surfaces and preview routes, including Race Prep, Debrief, and On the Water, while surfacing the data-model gaps captured later as follow-ups.

phases.title: Re-skin pass over the prior editorial surfaces
phases.summary: Shifted STEP_PALETTE values and related surface treatments from the editorial register to the iOS register across previously redesigned step surfaces.

phases.title: Surfaces 2–12 and preview inventory
phases.summary: Completed the preview build-out for all 12 iOS-register surfaces, including Playbook, Concept detail, Reflect, Discover, Get Inspired, Trophy, hinge, Auth Welcome, and Competency Assessment.

register_decisions.title: Register pivot
register_decisions.rationale: The user confirmed that the iOS register is canonical on native surfaces, so editorial tokens are superseded rather than coexisting behind a switch.

register_decisions.title: Permission rule schema deferred from the visual pass
register_decisions.rationale: The inline rule treatment is part of the visual register, but the underlying step_rules schema was explicitly deferred so UI work could ship with placeholder content first.

register_decisions.title: Per-interest beat naming keeps the existing plan-question spine
register_decisions.rationale: The migration should not introduce a parallel beats abstraction; the new BeatCard shell reuses the existing plan-question contract and maps naming per interest.

register_decisions.title: Lowercase eyebrow treatment reversed
register_decisions.rationale: The iOS register standardizes on ALL-CAPS 11px eyebrows, so the earlier lowercase eyebrow choice is explicitly reversed.

register_decisions.title: Reflect upgrade banner removes lavender
register_decisions.rationale: Coral remains reserved for AI-prompt and marked-content semantics, so the Reflect upgrade banner becomes a neutral white-on-gray treatment instead of a lavender info card.

register_decisions.title: Quote-card source provenance is limited to mic and bubble
register_decisions.rationale: Voice and written-note provenance can be represented with existing data, while AI-tagged lightbulb provenance remains deferred until the data layer supports it.

register_decisions.title: Faculty surfaces use denser layout calibration
register_decisions.rationale: Faculty-facing grading flows share the same token system as practitioner surfaces but compress spacing and title treatment to favor throughput over contemplative composition.

register_decisions.title: Competency rating segmented control gets an earned exception
register_decisions.rationale: The Competency Assessment segmented control is allowed to break the default 32px control rule because it carries the core grading decision and must read as a commitment.

architecture_decisions.title: Reflection vs Competency Assessment split
architecture_decisions.rationale: The After-phase is split into two artifacts: a practitioner-facing chronological Debrief reflection surface for narrative replay, and a separate faculty-primary Competency Assessment surface for structured evidence and grading. The existing mixed After tab should not continue as a single artifact.

architecture_decisions.title: Playbook home scope simplification
architecture_decisions.rationale: Playbook home should match the simplified iOS-register design rather than preserve all legacy first-order sections. Vision, concept shelf, recent reflections, and title block stay on the home surface; the rest move deeper so the screen reads as a library rather than a control panel.

architecture_decisions.title: Level-of-detail surfaces require distinct designs
architecture_decisions.rationale: Summary surfaces and detail surfaces do different jobs, so cards, shelves, and list items should not be miniature versions of full-screen step views. A summary treatment must be designed intentionally rather than engineered by shrinking the detail surface.

facets.title: RaceSummaryCard duality resolution
facets.rationale: Race Prep detail and its timeline-grid summary card must cut over together once the cards-view iOS design exists. Leaving the summary card in the legacy register while the detail view moves to iOS would create a seam across the same step, so StepDetailContent and RaceSummaryCard are treated as a coupled cutover.

legacy_retirement_blockers: Competency Assessment surface must land before the existing form-shaped After tab can retire

design_handoff_backlog.unblocks: Reflect home cutover without functionality regression

data_layer_followups.unblocks: Retiring the existing form-shaped After tab and completing the faculty-primary assessment flow
ConceptCard state pills, Concept detail state pill, and Race Prep WorkingOnPill state
Live-dot concept state in a step and the per-concept reflection trail on Concept detail
Real ForecastTileGroup conditions on Race Prep and other weather-aware surfaces
Reliable 'From your last race' quote stacks without text-shortening heuristics
external_dependencies: review_data.standout_quotes field or equivalent data model

data_layer_followups.unblocks: The inline 'FROM YOUR PLAYBOOK' coral AI prompt on Race Prep
Real Contingency rule rendering on Race Prep and On the Water
Clinical and future interest-specific naming on Race Prep and related step surfaces
external_dependencies: Product vocabulary decisions for non-sailing interests

data_layer_followups.unblocks: A complete Race Prep composition flow in the new register
external_dependencies: Decision on whether plan_data stays structured or becomes free-form prose

data_layer_followups.unblocks: Real return-count badges on Reflect home

cross_cutting_principles.name: Loading-state narration
cross_cutting_principles.description: Long-running AI or multi-step processing should narrate progress in plain language with swapping status lines instead of generic spinners or percentage-free indeterminate loaders.

cross_cutting_principles.name: Error-state principle
cross_cutting_principles.description: Every failure state should explain what happened in human terms and present a specific next action, using iOS-register attention chrome rather than technical dead-end errors.
